divergences.decision: ADR-002: Pluggable cluster-metadata backend keeps Metastore as the simple built-in option, while HA/production-critical metadata remains the ETCD posture; Metastore is documented as a Worker-0 coordination SPOF with no automatic failover.
divergences.detail: The PR introduces `dscli metastore_ha`, writes `metastore_head_nodes`, and updates docs/context to claim that `dscli up -f ./cluster_config_ha.json` starts every listed node as a Metastore service and keeps metadata available after a head failure. The diff does not update the actual deployment/runtime path: `cli/up.py` still reads only singular `metastore_head_node`, validates only that field, starts exactly one head first, and sets `start_metastore_service=true` only when the current node equals that singular head. Worker runtime still accepts a single `metastore_address` and starts one `MetaStoreServer` at that address. This turns a known non-HA Metastore mode into a documented HA posture without implementing the MM-required backend/failover/coordination change or updating the ADR decision.
divergences.evidence: cli/metastore_ha.py:new file hunks create `metastore_head_nodes` and remove `metastore_head_node`; docs/source_zh_cn/deployment/dscli.md:hunk adds claim that `dscli up` deploys each listed node and remains available after one head fails; docs/source_zh_cn/design_document/cluster_management.md:hunk adds multi-head HA Metastore section; .repo_context/modules/runtime/cluster-management.md:hunk records HA replicas; actual source check: cli/up.py still uses only `metastore_head_node` in _validate_metastore_config/process_node and src/datasystem/worker/worker_oc_server.cpp still uses singular FLAGS_metastore_address/start_metastore_service.
divergences.fix: Either implement and source-verify real multi-head Metastore semantics across config schema, `dscli up`, worker startup, client/backend address selection, replication/consensus/failover behavior, and tests; or scope this PR back to the existing single-head Metastore model and remove the HA/multi-head claims. If the architecture intentionally changes, update the Socratic ADR/MM to replace ADR-002's Metastore SPOF/ETCD-for-HA decision with the new design.
